museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: April 2001: Conker's Bad Fur Day gives N64 a rude late-life flourish.
museum.curatorNote: Initial researched draft for April 2001. Regional release dates are deliberately conservative and should be checked before verification.

sections.title: News
sections.subtitle: Five researched month markers or context notes.

items.title: Conker's Bad Fur Day gives N64 a rude late-life flourish.

items.body: A year-level context marker included to frame the month without claiming a new event happened on a specific day.
items.sourceNote: See source notes for this exhibit; exact dates should be rechecked during human edit.

items.title: PS2 moves from expensive launch object to obvious market leader.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: Gran Turismo 3: A-Spec
items.body: Polyphony makes PS2 screenshots look like a sales pitch for the future.
items.sourceNote: Japan/US/Europe timing varies across spring and summer 2001.

items.title: Phantasy Star Online
items.body: Sega makes online console RPG play feel briefly futuristic and communal.
items.sourceNote: Early 2001 western Dreamcast timing is source-noted; verify PAL date before final copy.

items.title: Black & White
items.body: Lionhead turns god-game promise into a creature you could discuss as if it were alive.
items.sourceNote: 2001 release lists place Black & White in March.

items.title: Conker's Bad Fur Day
items.body: Rare closes the N64's adult-comedy argument with technical polish and bad taste.
items.sourceNote: Regional timing varies; March/April 2001 is cautious.

items.title: Game Boy Advance launch line-up
items.body: Nintendo's handheld future begins with familiar names under a wider, brighter label.
items.sourceNote: European GBA launch was June 22, 2001; individual software timing needs title-by-title checking.

items.title: Max Payne
items.body: Bullet time leaves cinema and becomes a PC mechanic with a grim little snowstorm around it.
items.sourceNote: Commonly listed as July 2001 on PC.

items.title: Silent Hill 2
items.body: Konami turns PS2 horror inward, quieter and more adult.
items.sourceNote: Initial release timing is September 2001; regional dates vary.

items.title: Grand Theft Auto III
items.body: Liberty City becomes a new grammar for open-world games.
items.sourceNote: GTA III sources list October 2001; note the post-September delay context.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: Sega exits console hardware
items.body: The Dreamcast's production end turns Sega from platform-holder into third-party publisher.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot and contemporary reports cover Sega's January 2001 restructuring.

items.title: Game Boy Advance launches in Europe
items.body: Nintendo's handheld line becomes 32-bit, brighter in ambition and immediately familiar in the hand.
items.sourceNote: ZDNet reports the European launch date of June 22, 2001.

items.title: Xbox launches in North America
items.body: Microsoft enters console hardware with PC-like power and Halo as proof of life.
items.sourceNote: 2001 hardware references list November 15, 2001 for North America.

items.title: GameCube launches outside Europe
items.body: Nintendo's small-disc console arrives in Japan and North America while UK readers wait until 2002.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot E3 and 2001 references support the pre-European launch context.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Dial-up culture
items.body: Phantasy Star Online makes console online play feel intimate and strange, even as Sega's hardware future collapses.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period release context and UK access patterns; verify with period magazine/web sources before final publication.

items.title: Servers and services
items.body: PC players increasingly organise around servers, patches, clan pages, forums and downloaded maps.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period release context and UK access patterns; verify with period magazine/web sources before final publication.

items.title: Forums and files
items.body: Broadband is a rumour for many households; dial-up still shapes when and how long people can play online.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period release context and UK access patterns; verify with period magazine/web sources before final publication.

items.title: Console online experiments
items.body: GTA III discourse travels through school, shops, magazines and early web forums with unusual speed.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period release context and UK access patterns; verify with period magazine/web sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like a handover year. Sega's dream ended, Sony's PS2 settled in, Nintendo went handheld first, and Microsoft waited just offstage.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: You could love the Dreamcast and still know the shop window had moved on. That was the ache of 2001.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: The Game Boy Advance made old habits new again: link cables, batteries, playground comparisons and a screen you tried to angle toward the light.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: By Christmas, European players were living in previews of Xbox and GameCube while PS2 did the actual work under the television.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
